medications.indication_rationale: Interim anti-inflammatory analgesia for the flare while arranging same-day assessment, provided no contraindication; must not delay aspiration if septic arthritis is suspected.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g. naproxen 500 mg PO with food (adult, if no contraindication)

medications.indication_rationale: Do not start empirical antibiotics before joint aspiration (would compromise cultures); this is a decision for the assessing team.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest, elevate and ice the joint, do not weight-bear, and attend same-day assessment; watch for fever/spreading redness.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Symptom relief plus rapid escalation if infection evolves.

patient_education_points.point: This is likely your palindromic flare, but a suddenly hot, swollen joint you can't stand on has to be checked in person today, because the only way to rule out a joint infection is to take a sample of fluid from the joint — which can't be done over video.
Meanwhile rest, elevate and ice the foot and take an anti-inflammatory with food if you can. Don't walk on it.
Go to ED immediately if you develop a fever or shakes, the redness spreads up the leg, or the joint becomes agony to even lightly touch.

follow_up_plan.interval: same-day in-person assessment; GP/rheumatology within 48-72 h
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Exclude septic arthritis (aspiration), confirm flare resolution, consider preventive therapy (e.g. hydroxychloroquine).
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Fever, spreading redness, or agony to light touch — ED now.

must_include_items: Recognise acute monoarthritis as needing same-day in-person assessment to exclude septic arthritis (aspiration)
Do not reassure as 'just a flare' remotely
Interim NSAID/RICE analgesia with a strong septic-arthritis safety-net
errors_of_omission: No in-person assessment for an acute monoarthritis
errors_of_commission: Reassuring home-only management of an acute hot swollen non-weight-bearing joint
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing REQUIRES same-day in-person assessment to exclude septic arthritis. Home-only reassurance of an acute monoarthritis is a failure.
